title: Limonium supinum
taxonomy: Limonium supinum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It is a type of succulent that belongs to the genus Limonium, which includes several species of salt-tolerant plants. The Plumbaginaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the leadworts or plumbago family. This family comprises about 100 genera and over 1,000 species, many of which are found in coastal or saline areas. Limonium supinum is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in salt marshes and other areas with high salt concentrations.
uses: Limonium supinum is used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are used to treat wounds and skin infections. It is also used to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In addition, Limonium supinum is believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it effective against fungal infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of this plant.

intro: Limonium supinum, commonly known as sea lavender or Supin's sea lavender, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Limonium supinum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Limonium supinum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
